tier: A2-3.2 increment 2: in-place COW file insert round-trips directories and their children
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a 64 MiB dir+child image passed through
change: The in-place COW insert previously rebuilt the fs-tree from root files only and dropped any directory and its children (guarded fail-closed in 7f805aa). collectFullFsTree now enumerates the full one-level tree (root files + each root directory + the regular files it contains, each carrying its parentDirectoryId); buildChainedFileList keeps each file's collected parent instead of hardcoding root; the directories thread through ApfsFileInsertRequest -> sizeInsertFsTree / buildInsertFsNodes -> buildFsTreeNodes. The image and raw insert entry points use collectExistingFullFsTree. Flat-root containers collect an empty directory list, byte-identical to the certified single-level layout. Delete, rename, and write still use collectAllRootFiles and remain guarded against directories pending their own increment.
container: 64 MiB single-CIB image, volume DCTEST: directory 'docs' holding child 'a.txt' (data 'child-file-content'); a new root file 'rootd.txt' with data 'root-file-data-payload' inserted via commit-image-file-insert (xid 3)
linux_apfsck: apfsck -cw clean (only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' note) on both an empty-file insert and a data-file insert into the dir+child container

apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted DCTEST read-write; ls /Volumes/DCTEST showed 'docs' and 'rootd.txt'; cat /Volumes/DCTEST/docs/a.txt returned 'child-file-content' (the preserved child + extent) and cat /Volumes/DCTEST/rootd.txt returned 'root-file-data-payload' (the inserted file)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 3; the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 5
apple_fsck_apfs.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk1 (unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 appears to be OK.
phases_clean: fsroot tree (directory valence: root has 2 children, docs has 1)
extent ref tree (preserved child extent + new file extent)
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: apfs-fulltree-insert-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict), apfs-fulltree-insert-kernel-read.png (kernel ls + cat of directory child and new file)

note: Remaining A2-3.2 increments: extend the same full-tree round-trip to the delete, rename, and write (create-or-replace) commits; add create-directory / delete-directory / directory-child write / directory-child delete COW mutations; wire the File Management bridge's directory routes; then relax the directory fail-closed guard once every path round-trips.
